main.welcome: Welcome to my business card website.
main.im: I'm a front-end developer who loves to create something new and different.

footer.info: I've been working in software development for five years, and with each project, I strive to implement new technologies and stay updated on industry trends. This helps me remain at the forefront of innovative solutions.

ic8.first: Developed a new product "Admin Panel" for managing tariffs and online tariff's settings
ic8.second: Improved the product for service requests and team management
ic8.third: Developed a new web version of the broadband internet tariff plan product, implementing Tanstack Query technology

fgk.first: Development and design of user interfaces for manager and client dashboards using Vue.js.
fgk.second: Implemented a responsive, intuitive design that improves user experience and increases manager productivity.
fgk.third: Independent development and optimization of the backend. Improved server-side architecture, optimized database queries and API, ensuring high performance and system reliability.
fgk.fourth: Creation of an integrated development environment (IDE) using Vue.js for internal use by the management team. This platform accelerated the calculation process.

qwep.first: Complete redesign and launch of a new web version of platform applications, completed in a team with a fellow developer. The project involved rewriting the applications from scratch, resulting in improved performance, a modern user interface, and greater system scalability.
qwep.second: Development of additional functionality for applications as an ongoing task.

digital.first: Developed and launched work schedule features with flexible views, filters, and search.
digital.second: Built a Draft.js text editor for complex reporting.
digital.third: Improved the design system (UI Kit) and participated in migration to microfrontends.

revoluterra.first: Built SEO applications in React and launched the PRO version in Vue.
revoluterra.second: Redesigned UX/UI for result tables and onboarding.
revoluterra.third: Implemented keyword recommendations and Excel export.
revoluterra.fourth: Created charts and collaborated across teams.

aboutMe.name: Name:
aboutMe.aiValue: Completed the “AI for Developers” course and received a diploma.
aboutMe.aiProject: Built a Telegram bot for online communities: reducing toxic communication, launching IT quizzes, helping resolve disputes, assigning participant ranks, and improving engagement.
aboutMe.growthValue: I lead an educational stream, help grow the team, and continuously improve as an engineer and leader.
aboutMe.intro: Frontend developer with solid commercial experience and strong focus on product outcomes, UX, and engineering quality.
aboutMe.current: I work with React/Vue/TypeScript, improve architecture, design systems, and team delivery processes.
aboutMe.bio1: I have been in IT for over 5 years. It all started with my love for physics and math, and evolved into a JavaScript developer diploma and a passion for building complex interfaces.
aboutMe.bio2: My development journey is all about momentum and continuous learning. At Federal Freight Company (FGK), I worked not only on manager dashboards in Vue, but also went under the hood of the backend with PHP and Laravel. Later, within the QWEP project, our team rewrote the app from scratch and successfully launched a new React-based web version.
aboutMe.bio4: 2025: Recognized as “Frontend Developer of the Year” at my primary workplace.
aboutMe.bio5: 2026: Deep-dived into neural networks and completed the “AI for Developers” course.
aboutMe.bio6: To turn theory into practice, I built an AI bot for my Telegram channel. It not only engages the audience with IT quizzes and activities, but also analyzes chat sentiment to help maintain a healthy community atmosphere.
aboutMe.bio7: Now I continue building complex frontend solutions, integrating AI tools into workflows, and, as my colleagues say, proudly carrying the title “mother of react” ©.
aboutMe.journeyTitle: Path in Code: From Logic to Interfaces
aboutMe.journeyText: It all started with a physics-math background and a love for exact sciences. This foundation helped me quickly move from first lines of code to a JavaScript developer diploma. My drive in the profession is constant learning and working at the intersection of technologies.
aboutMe.expIntro: In 5+ years of development, I have worked on projects of different complexity:
aboutMe.expScaleText: Within the QWEP project, our team rewrote the app from scratch and successfully launched a new React-based web version.
aboutMe.expFullstackText: At Federal Freight Company (FGK), I not only built Vue dashboards but also worked under the hood with PHP and Laravel, independently improving backend logic and building an IDE.
aboutMe.aiEraText: In 2026, I integrated neural networks into my workflow, confirming my expertise with an “AI for Developers” diploma. To reinforce theory with practice, I built a smart AI bot for my IT Telegram channel. It became a full participant in the community: runs IT quizzes, adds activities, and analyzes chat sentiment.
aboutMe.featureLogicText: It started with a physics-math background and a love for exact sciences. This foundation helped me quickly move from first lines of code to a JavaScript developer diploma.
aboutMe.featureProductText: Within the QWEP project, our team rewrote the product from scratch and successfully launched a new web version on React. At FGC, I built dashboards in Vue and worked with backend in PHP/Laravel, so I am confident with both frontend and backend-adjacent tasks.
aboutMe.featureFutureText: In 2026, I confirmed my expertise with an “AI for Developers” diploma and integrated AI into my workflow. I also built a smart bot, Oski, for my IT channel: it runs quizzes, boosts activity, and analyzes chat sentiment.
aboutMe.mainPreview1: I have been in IT for over 5 years. It started with a love for physics and math, and evolved into the title “mother of react” © and a passion for complex interface architecture.
aboutMe.mainPreview2: I progressed from building my own IDEs to large-scale refactoring of major web services. I continuously expand my stack: from deep React and Vue expertise to integrating AI tools into real products.
aboutMe.mainPreview3:
Current highlights:
🏆 2025: “Frontend Developer of the Year”
🤖 2026: Certified AI engineer and creator of a smart community assistant
aboutMe.mainPreviewMore: Learn more about my journey and projects →

card.ic8Do: In 2024, I developed an application for technical specialists to manage teams and launched a new web version of the broadband internet product.
card.fgkDo: I developed manager and client dashboards, independently refined the backend. I discovered Vue in commercial development and wrote an IDE using it.
card.qwepDo: In 2023, we rewrote the platform applications from scratch and successfully launched a new web version. I continue to develop QWEP application functionality in addition to my main work.
card.digitalDo: Work schedules, Draft.js text editor, UI Kit, microfrontends migration, planning & mentoring.
card.revoluterraDo: SEO apps in React, PRO in Vue, UX/UI redesign, keyword recommendations, Excel export, charts, mentoring.

story.work: «You know that you will be a mega senor»
story.shiba: Ruler of the local digital lands and fox
story.croatia: Main life goal is to move to Croatia

Work.start: I was born in Moscow on Friday the 13th and spent my entire childhood working with my brothers on computers. The eldest of us is a computer science teacher.
Work.education: At school I studied in the physics and mathematics class. I received my higher education at the Russian Cooperation University, defending my diploma with excellent marks.
Work.js: I also successfully completed training at a programming school, graduating with a diploma in JavaScript developer.
Work.frontend: Currently I have been developing for about four years. I am happy to take on additional projects and constantly develop myself.
Work.lib: My first personal project was an electronic library. While searching for a job, I needed to keep myself busy, so I started writing.
Work.friend: This idea was suggested by my friends:
Work.whantLib: «We want to post our books online and also book them!».
Work.app: The application allowed you to register, post your own book to the public, find and book the one you were interested in. You could write a comment on the book or simply leave a rating.
Work.deploy: I developed the design myself. Created an interface without using UI libraries. I connected the database, rolled out the project to Heroku and voila.
Work.offer: The first offer is in my pocket!
Work.dontStop: Not stopping there, I continued to study new technologies and create cool things.
Work.fgk: One of the interesting and important experiences for me was working at the Federal Freight Company (Main subsidiary of Russian Railways).
Work.backend: At the FFC I developed accounts for managers and clients and I was lucky enough to learn PHP and Laravel and independently develop the back-end.
Work.vue: I opened Vue in commercial development and wrote an IDE using it.
Work.qwep: Now I have returned to React and, in addition to my main job, I continue to help develop the functionality of the QWEP application. In 2023, we rewrote applications from scratch and successfully rolled out

Kanal.name: name
Kanal.kanal: No matter how loud it may sound, the most important thing I have now is my channel. My brainchild!
Kanal.save: One day I came up with the idea of taking notes in a telegram. I created a channel for myself and saved useful IT links, articles, and interesting projects there. I made some footnotes for myself and wrote comments.
Kanal.story: I suddenly discovered that the channel was starting to gain subscribers and decided at that moment that in the process of gaining experience I would write down my stories, achievements and events from life at work there.
Kanal.qweez: By the 500 subscriber mark, the channel’s audience became absolutely clear to me, and even a certain community had formed. We got local memes, we started holding quizzes on the channel, including from subscribers, we study different programming languages every day.
Kanal.memes: Every Wednesday we have It's Wednesday, my dudes, and on Friday Friday day is the day when subscribers send their memes in the comments. Yes, yes, only on Friday! Otherwise they will be arrested :)
Kanal.qween: Subscribers began to call me the Queen of Dudes, thank me endlessly and write letters of love for the fact that we have this amazing place.
Kanal.people: The channel united not only juniors, but also experienced programmers writing in a variety of languages. Among the subscribers there are students who are just studying. Also, despite the theme of the channel, there are people here from completely different areas who are simply interested in watching memes and communicating, and for some, my experience is motivation for changes in life.
Kanal.stickers: At the end of 2023, I created a sticker pack with my puppy. My Oski became the ambassador of the channel and everyone’s favorite, and quickly got fans. It turned out to be a great success: to date, more than 4,000 Telegram users have installed the stickers, and they have been used more than 150,000 times!

Doggy.name: groundhopper
Doggy.dog: «Sorry, why was I a groundhopper - because I didn’t have a dog!»
Doggy.gh: Let's start with what it is - groundhopping and what eat it with.
Doggy.beer: German readers of my site would now tell you: with beer and sausages!
Doggy.stadiums: Groundhopping is football travel without being tied to a specific club. When choosing a match, such travelers usually take into account how interesting the game will be: whether the teams will have fans to listen to their songs, whether the history of the club is fascinating, and of course the beauty of the stadium itself.
Doggy.football: Few people are interested in football itself in such a story, but I know groundhoppers who really watch the match from the first to the last minute and evaluate the game itself and worry :)
Doggy.club: This is logical, after all, we all gathered for one reason - football, so intuitively for one hour you begin to “sink” for the club that is more attractive to you.
Doggy.groundhopper: Where does the groundhopper come from?
Doggy.tired: As a rule, this is a football fan who, throughout his life, has traveled to many cities and countries for his club, and each trip has already become tiresome and uninteresting.
Doggy.cities: Nothing changes every year: St. Petersburg, Tula, Rostov, Samara, Kazan...
Doggy.hobby: I really like each of these cities, but I simply have nothing to see in them anymore. This is how you come up with a new activity related to your favorite hobby - groundhopping.
Doggy.loko: A couple of years ago I went to St. Petersburg for my Lokomotiv team, and returned to Loko’s game in Khimki without visiting Moscow (essentially a double).
Doggy.matches: During this trip I attended 10 football matchesin the Leningrad and Tver regions.
Doggy.book: My article about one of these matches was published in a book on groundhopping.
Doggy.stat: To date, I have visited more than a hundred unique football stadiums with matches there, and I am among the top 100 groundhoppers in Russia. In 2021, I joined the Russian Groundhopper League.
Doggy.buy: But! As my former boss put it«A woman bought a pig!»
Doggy.fox: In August 2023, I became the happy owner of a small red fox. And this is where my travels sparkled with new colors.
Doggy.meet: In no time at all, I began introducing Oscar to football. I created an Oski account on the website groundhopper.ru and a new quest began.
Doggy.dogFriendly: Now I need to rely not only on the above-mentioned factors when choosing a game, but also focus on Dog Friendly stadiums, and, unfortunately, there are practically no such ones :(
Doggy.metallist: The first match in Oscar's life was the game of Metalist from the city of Korolev. Despite signs posted throughout the stadium prohibiting dogs from attending games, Oski entered the territory freely and was able to watch the football.
Doggy.belarus: My hobby has become even more exciting. I have an ideal dog for traveling, and Oski has already traveled with me to the neighboring country - Belarus.
Doggy.like: The most amazing thing is that I see that my fox really likes it all.

Croatia.name: goal
Croatia.love: The first time I came to Croatia was in 2016 and from that moment I fell in love with this country. I really like the climate of Croatia, the mentality of these people, their love for football. The country suits me very well.
Croatia.zg80: In 2020, I translated from Croatian into Russian the film ZG80, about the trip of Dinamo Zagreb fans to Serbia for a game with Red Star.
Croatia.friends: Over all these years I have made friends in Zagreb, Split, Osijek, Dubrovnik. I learned the Croatian language, I even studied with a tutor.
Croatia.twitter: On Twitter, I became friends with the Lokomotiva Zagreb football club, and the SMM manager from the club’s account liked my posts about Lokomotiva :)
Croatia.dreams: In 2023, I decided to actively move, but my plans were dashed by searching for a job in Croatia.
Croatia.work: In February 2024, I applied for a vacancy for a Frontend developer at an IT company from Zagreb. The required technology stack was ideal for mine. A couple of days later I received a non-disclosure agreement and an invitation to an interview in the mail.
Croatia.happy: I was incredibly happy and prepared literally days and nights. I was able to talk in Croatian about event-loop, about closures, about everything in the world.
Croatia.interview: It was a very interesting interview. We talked about my experience, about technology, there were psychological questions. The entire interview lasted 40 minutes and was in Croatian. At the end, HR praised me very much, but she was confused that I was in Russia, and that my move could take 4 months, according to her experience. I received a refusal.
Croatia.sad: I was very upset, but decided that I would continue to try. At the time of my job search, I had already found housing in Zagreb near the Lokomotiva stadium, but it turned out to be difficult to find work in Croatia.
